name: pymoo
tagline: Reference/how-to skill for pymoo, the Python multi-objective optimization library (NSGA-II/III, MOEA/D, Pareto fronts).
verdict: Clean, purely instructional documentation skill with no security red flags: no credential access, no network exfiltration, no piped remote-code execution, no hidden instructions. Only real issue is the file runs 657 lines, over the ~500-line hygiene budget, and lacks an explicit negative-trigger note.

highlights.summary: No explicit 'do NOT use when' / negative-trigger guidance anywhere in the file; a user could misapply it to single-variable calculus optimization or non-Python contexts without being told otherwise.
File is 657 lines, well above the ~500-line hygiene budget, though the length is spent on genuinely distinct workflows rather than padding.
